title: store: create_group_with_creator_capped does not check rows_affected on INSERT
description: In create_group_with_creator_capped (store.rs ~line 1676), after passing the cap check the INSERT OR IGNORE into groups and INSERT OR IGNORE into group_members are issued. If the group_id already exists (a retry/race), the INSERT OR IGNORE silently returns rows_affected=0 but the function returns Ok(true) — incorrectly telling the caller the group was newly created. This misleads the caller into sending a GROUP_CREATED notification for an already-existing group. Trigger: two near-simultaneous GROUP_CREATE requests with the same group_id where the first commits between the cap check and the insert of the second.
